title: Fuel Cell Stocks Rally: FCEL, PLUG, and BE Surge on AI Data Center Demand
text: FuelCell Energy (FCEL) surged 18% in midday trading, with Plug Power climbing 13% and Bloom Energy rallying 12%. FCEL traded around $16.41, up from Friday's $13.70 close. Primary driver is intense hyperscaler power consumption. Hyperscaler power needs have outpaced what utilities can deliver, with grid interconnection queues stretching 5-7 years. This forces data centers to seek on-site generation solutions that fuel cell companies can provide. Bloom Energy demonstrated strongest fundamentals, reporting Q1 2026 revenue of $751.05 million (up 130% YoY) with $20 billion backlog. Management noted: bring-your-own-power has shifted from slogan to business necessity for AI hyperscalers. The move reflects sector rotation rather than defensive positioning, with VIX at 17.19. Market sentiment driven by AI infrastructure growth but article warns fuel cell sector has long history of parabolic rallies followed by sharp giveback when execution lags expectations. Key risks: Bloom Energy trades at premium 128x forward earnings, and FCEL remains substantially down over past decade, reflecting capital intensity and dilution challenges. However, AI-driven data center power demand represents significant growth opportunity for fuel cell technology companies.
